name: Lamb Slivers in Pungent Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 large Bell pepper, trimmed and sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the lamb across the grain into slivers about 2 inches long.
2. In a bowl, sprinkle the lamb with salt and cornstarch; rub the mixture into the meat and add sherry. Marinate for 15 minutes.
3. Trim scallions into 2-inch pieces and halve the bell pepper, removing cores and slicing to match the lamb.
4. Combine stock, soy sauce, sugar, and vinegar in a separate bowl.
5. Heat peanut oil in a wok until it reaches deep-fry temperature (bubbles form around a dry chopstick held upright in the oil).
6. Fry the lamb slivers briefly until a coating of starch begins to brown. Remove them to a strainer and set aside.
7. Remove the oil from the wok and discard it.
8. Return the wok to high heat and add 2 tablespoons of oil.
9. When the oil starts to smoke, add the scallions and bell pepper. Stir-fry for 30 seconds.
10. Add garlic and ginger. Continue stir-frying until the peppers turn bright green.
11. Pour in the stock mixture and stir until the liquid boils and reduces slightly.
12. Add the lamb back into the wok. Stir-fry until the lamb is hot. Serve.
